back_translation: When Jesus had spoken these things, He went out with His disciples across the Kidron Brook. There was a garden there, and He and His disciples entered it.
Judas, who betrayed Him, also knew that place, because Jesus had often met with His disciples there.
So Judas brought a band of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees there, with lanterns, torches, and weapons.
Jesus knew everything that was about to happen to Him, so He went out and said to them, “Whom are you coming to find?”
They answered Him, “Jesus the Nazarene.” He said, “I am the One.” And Judas, who betrayed Him, was also standing with them.
When He said, “I am the One,” they drew back and fell to the ground.
So He asked them again, “Whom are you coming to find?” They said, “Jesus the Nazarene.”
Jesus answered, “I have already told you that I am the One. So if you are coming for Me, let these men go.”
This was so that the word He had spoken would be fulfilled: ‘Of those whom You have entrusted to Me, I have not lost a single one.’
Simon Peter had a sword, so he drew it out and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.
Then Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword in its sheath. Shall I not drink the cup the Father has given Me?”
So the band of soldiers, the commander, and the officers of the Jews seized Jesus and tied Him up.
They took Him first to Annas, because Annas was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was the high priest that year.
Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jews that it would be better if one man died on behalf of the people.
Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. That disciple was known to the high priest, so he went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.
But Peter stood outside at the door. So the other disciple who was known to the high priest came out and spoke to the woman gatekeeper, then brought Peter in.
The servant girl who was the gatekeeper asked Peter, “Aren’t you also a disciple of this man?” Peter answered, “I am not.”
Because it was cold, the slaves and officers had made a charcoal fire and were standing warming themselves. And Peter was also standing with them, warming himself.
At that time, the high priest questioned Jesus about His disciples and His teaching.
Jesus answered, “I have spoken to the people of the world openly. I have always taught in the synagogues and at the temple, where the Jews come together. I have said nothing in secret.
Why are you questioning Me? Ask those who heard what I said to them. Look, those people know what I have said.”
When He said this, one of the officers standing nearby slapped Jesus’ face and said, “Is this the way You answer the high priest?”
Jesus answered him, “If I spoke wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I spoke rightly, why do you strike Me?”
So Annas sent Him, still tied up, to Caiaphas the high priest.
Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing and warming himself. So they asked him, “Aren’t you also one of His disciples?” Peter denied it and said, “I am not.”
One of the high priest’s slaves, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, asked, “Didn’t I see you with Him in the garden?”
So Peter denied it again, and immediately the rooster crowed.

Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas’ place to the governor’s headquarters. By that time it was early morning. They themselves did not enter the headquarters, so that they would not become defiled, but might eat the Passover.
So Pilate came out to them and asked, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”
They answered, “If this man were not a doer of evil, we would not have handed him over to you.”
Pilate then said to them, “You take him and judge him according to your own law.” The Jews replied, “We are not permitted to put anyone to death.”
This was so that the word of Jesus would be fulfilled — the word He had spoken to indicate the manner of death He was going to die.
So Pilate went back into the headquarters, summoned Jesus, and asked Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”
Jesus answered, “Are you saying this from your own thinking, or did others tell you about Me?”
Pilate answered, “Am I a Jew? Your nation and the chief priests have handed you over to me. What have you done?”

Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If My kingdom were of this world, My royal-officials would have fought to prevent Me from being handed over to the Jews. But now My kingdom is not from here.”

So Pilate said to Him, “So then, are You a king or not?” Jesus answered, “You yourself say that I am a king. For this reason I was born, and for this reason I came into the world — that I might testify to the truth. Everyone who is on the side of truth listens to My voice.”
Pilate said to Him, “What is truth?” Having said this, he went out again to the Jews and said to them, “I find no guilt in him at all.”
But you have a custom that I would release one prisoner to you at the Passover festival. So do you want me to release the King of the Jews to you?”
So they shouted again, saying, “Not this one — release Barabbas instead!” Now Barabbas was a robber-insurrectionist.
